{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大社）、旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = 矢落神社、矢落明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月9日、10日 |神事 = 9月20日・航空祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=矢田坐久志
玉比古神社|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|楼門に奉納されているプロペラ]] '''矢田坐久志玉比古神社'''（やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}{{jalink|矢田村 (奈良県)|Q17173675|矢田町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。大社に列せられる{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。別名、矢落（やおち）神社、矢落明神[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage ロコどこ 観光ナビ 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]と呼ばれる。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - 久志玉比古神（{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}）、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}} === 歴史 === 当社の詳しい創建年代は不明であるが、当社の祭神である{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}は、{{jalink|天磐船|Q126914107}}に乗って空を飛んだと{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}に記されている。その際に櫛玉饒速日神が天から放った2本目の矢が落ちた所に当社が建てられたとされている。また、これによってこの地を矢田というようになったという。 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）の諸社神封の条には「矢田神二戸 {{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}」とあり、当社はそれ以前から建立されていた古社であることがわかる奈良県史閲覧。なお、{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の皇后である{{jalink|八田皇女|Q10892311|矢田皇女}}はこの地方の出身と考えられている式内社の研究 第2巻より。 当社は、当地方最大の古社として創建より6世紀前半までは{{jalink|畿内|Q906708}}随一の名社として栄えたとされている。また、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載されている{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}十座の筆頭社で{{jalink|式内社||式内大社}}である。 {{jalink|1879年|Q7783}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年）の神社明細帳では当社の祭神は{{jalink|天太玉命}}となっている。どうやら{{jalink|明治政府}}の圧力で40年程天太玉命を祀らされたらようである。 明治時代に郷社に列せられているが、{{jalink|1938年|Q18645}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}13年）10月には{{jalink|県社}}に格上げされている。 櫛玉饒速日命が天磐船に乗って空を飛んだとの故事により、櫛玉饒速日命は航空祖神として崇められており毎年9月20日には航空祭が行われている[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html 大和郡山市観光協会 観光案内 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。楼門の上層部にはかつて{{jalink|戦闘機|Q127771}}に使用されていたという{{jalink|プロペラ|Q922654}}が奉納され、飾られている。 境内には二之矢塚がある。また、この地こそ{{jalink|邪馬台国|Q844295}}であるという説もある。 === 境内 === * 本殿（{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}） - {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}初期の再建。一間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}、檜皮葺[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html 大和郡山市 文化財 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。 * 神門 * 拝殿 * 二之矢塚 * 楼門 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === * {{jalink|八幡神社}}（重要文化財） - 祭神：{{jalink|誉田別命}}。室町時代中期の再建。 * {{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} - 祭神：{{jalink|宇摩志麻遅命||宇摩志麻治命}}、{{jalink|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|弥都波能売神||弥都波能賣神}} * 境外社 ** 主人（ぬしと）神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}}。または御炊屋姫命、{{jalink|天比理刀咩命|Q85879124}}（あめのひりとめのみこと）の説があるが、天比理刀咩命は明治政府による圧力で一時期に祀られていただけのようである。境外社であるとともに{{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}}でもある。御旅所でもあったことを鑑みると、饒速日命、御炊屋姫命の二座のようである神主様宛確認。 ** {{jalink|八阪神社}}：祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男||須佐之男命}}。現在は独立している。 === 文化財 === === 重要文化財 === * 矢田坐久志玉比古神社 2棟{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=重要文化財一覧 |publisher=大和郡山市 |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** 本殿 ** 末社八幡神社社殿 === 祭事 === * 9月20日 - 航空祭 * 10月9日、10日 - 例祭 === 所在地 === * 奈良県大和郡山市矢田町965 === アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066|橿原線}} {{jalink|近鉄郡山駅|Q6414349}}からバス「大和小泉駅東口」行き、または「{{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}}前」行きに乗車し「横山口」で下車後、徒歩約6分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = 池田末則ほか |editor = 谷川健一編 |title = 日本の神々 : 神社と聖地 第4巻 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|櫛玉命神社|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|磐船神社 (交野市)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|船山神社|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|石船神社 (村上市)|Q124496786}} === 外部リンク === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage ロコどこ 観光ナビ 矢田坐久志玉比古神社] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

久志玉比古神（{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}）、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}} === 歴史 === 当社の詳しい創建年代は不明であるが、当社の祭神である{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}は、{{jalink|天磐船|Q126914107}}に乗って空を飛んだと{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}に記されている。その際に櫛玉饒速日神が天から放った2本目の矢が落ちた所に当社が建てられたとされている。また、これによってこの地を矢田というようになったという。 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）の諸社神封の条には「矢田神二戸 {{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}」とあり、当社はそれ以前から建立されていた古社であることがわかる奈良県史閲覧。なお、{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の皇后である{{jalink|八田皇女|Q10892311|矢田皇女}}はこの地方の出身と考えられている式内社の研究 第2巻より。 当社は、当地方最大の古社として創建より6世紀前半までは{{jalink|畿内|Q906708}}随一の名社として栄えたとされている。また、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載されている{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}十座の筆頭社で{{jalink|式内社||式内大社}}である。 {{jalink|1879年|Q7783}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年）の神社明細帳では当社の祭神は{{jalink|天太玉命}}となっている。どうやら{{jalink|明治政府}}の圧力で40年程天太玉命を祀らされたらようである。 明治時代に郷社に列せられているが、{{jalink|1938年|Q18645}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}13年）10月には{{jalink|県社}}に格上げされている。 櫛玉饒速日命が天磐船に乗って空を飛んだとの故事により、櫛玉饒速日命は航空祖神として崇められており毎年9月20日には航空祭が行われている[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html 大和郡山市観光協会 観光案内 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。楼門の上層部にはかつて{{jalink|戦闘機|Q127771}}に使用されていたという{{jalink|プロペラ|Q922654}}が奉納され、飾られている。 境内には二之矢塚がある。また、この地こそ{{jalink|邪馬台国|Q844295}}であるという説もある。 === 境内 === * 本殿（{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}） - {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}初期の再建。一間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}、檜皮葺[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html 大和郡山市 文化財 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。 * 神門 * 拝殿 * 二之矢塚 * 楼門 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === * {{jalink|八幡神社}}（重要文化財） - 祭神：{{jalink|誉田別命}}。室町時代中期の再建。 * {{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} - 祭神：{{jalink|宇摩志麻遅命||宇摩志麻治命}}、{{jalink|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|弥都波能売神||弥都波能賣神}} * 境外社 ** 主人（ぬしと）神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}}。または御炊屋姫命、{{jalink|天比理刀咩命|Q85879124}}（あめのひりとめのみこと）の説があるが、天比理刀咩命は明治政府による圧力で一時期に祀られていただけのようである。境外社であるとともに{{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}}でもある。御旅所でもあったことを鑑みると、饒速日命、御炊屋姫命の二座のようである神主様宛確認。 ** {{jalink|八阪神社}}：祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男||須佐之男命}}。現在は独立している。 === 文化財 === === 重要文化財 === * 矢田坐久志玉比古神社 2棟{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=重要文化財一覧 |publisher=大和郡山市 |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** 本殿 ** 末社八幡神社社殿 === 祭事 === * 9月20日 - 航空祭 * 10月9日、10日 - 例祭 === 所在地 === * 奈良県大和郡山市矢田町965 === アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066|橿原線}} {{jalink|近鉄郡山駅|Q6414349}}からバス「大和小泉駅東口」行き、または「{{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}}前」行きに乗車し「横山口」で下車後、徒歩約6分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = 池田末則ほか |editor = 谷川健一編 |title = 日本の神々 : 神社と聖地 第4巻 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|櫛玉命神社|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|磐船神社 (交野市)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|船山神社|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|石船神社 (村上市)|Q124496786}} === 外部リンク === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage ロコどこ 観光ナビ 矢田坐久志玉比古神社] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

玉比古神社|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|楼門に奉納されているプロペラ]] '''矢田坐久志玉比古神社'''（やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}{{jalink|矢田村 (奈良県)|Q17173675|矢田町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。大社に列せられる{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。別名、矢落（やおち）神社、矢落明神[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage ロコどこ 観光ナビ 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]と呼ばれる。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - 久志玉比古神（{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}）、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}} === 歴史 === 当社の詳しい創建年代は不明であるが、当社の祭神である{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|櫛玉饒速日命}}は、{{jalink|天磐船|Q126914107}}に乗って空を飛んだと{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}に記されている。その際に櫛玉饒速日神が天から放った2本目の矢が落ちた所に当社が建てられたとされている。また、これによってこの地を矢田というようになったという。 『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）の諸社神封の条には「矢田神二戸 {{jalink|大和国|Q907495|大和}}」とあり、当社はそれ以前から建立されていた古社であることがわかる奈良県史閲覧。なお、{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}の皇后である{{jalink|八田皇女|Q10892311|矢田皇女}}はこの地方の出身と考えられている式内社の研究 第2巻より。 当社は、当地方最大の古社として創建より6世紀前半までは{{jalink|畿内|Q906708}}随一の名社として栄えたとされている。また、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載されている{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}十座の筆頭社で{{jalink|式内社||式内大社}}である。 {{jalink|1879年|Q7783}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年）の神社明細帳では当社の祭神は{{jalink|天太玉命}}となっている。どうやら{{jalink|明治政府}}の圧力で40年程天太玉命を祀らされたらようである。 明治時代に郷社に列せられているが、{{jalink|1938年|Q18645}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}13年）10月には{{jalink|県社}}に格上げされている。 櫛玉饒速日命が天磐船に乗って空を飛んだとの故事により、櫛玉饒速日命は航空祖神として崇められており毎年9月20日には航空祭が行われている[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html 大和郡山市観光協会 観光案内 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。楼門の上層部にはかつて{{jalink|戦闘機|Q127771}}に使用されていたという{{jalink|プロペラ|Q922654}}が奉納され、飾られている。 境内には二之矢塚がある。また、この地こそ{{jalink|邪馬台国|Q844295}}であるという説もある。 === 境内 === * 本殿（{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}） - {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}初期の再建。一間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}、檜皮葺[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html 大和郡山市 文化財 矢田坐久志玉比古神社]。 * 神門 * 拝殿 * 二之矢塚 * 楼門 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === * {{jalink|八幡神社}}（重要文化財） - 祭神：{{jalink|誉田別命}}。室町時代中期の再建。 * {{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} - 祭神：{{jalink|宇摩志麻遅命||宇摩志麻治命}}、{{jalink|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|弥都波能売神||弥都波能賣神}} * 境外社 ** 主人（ぬしと）神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}、{{jalink|ミカシキヤヒメ|Q11341880|御炊屋姫命}}。または御炊屋姫命、{{jalink|天比理刀咩命|Q85879124}}（あめのひりとめのみこと）の説があるが、天比理刀咩命は明治政府による圧力で一時期に祀られていただけのようである。境外社であるとともに{{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}}でもある。御旅所でもあったことを鑑みると、饒速日命、御炊屋姫命の二座のようである神主様宛確認。 ** {{jalink|八阪神社}}：祭神：{{jalink|須佐之男||須佐之男命}}。現在は独立している。 === 文化財 === === 重要文化財 === * 矢田坐久志玉比古神社 2棟{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=重要文化財一覧 |publisher=大和郡山市 |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** 本殿 ** 末社八幡神社社殿 === 祭事 === * 9月20日 - 航空祭 * 10月9日、10日 - 例祭 === 所在地 === * 奈良県大和郡山市矢田町965 === アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066|橿原線}} {{jalink|近鉄郡山駅|Q6414349}}からバス「大和小泉駅東口」行き、または「{{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}}前」行きに乗車し「横山口」で下車後、徒歩約6分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = 池田末則ほか |editor = 谷川健一編 |title = 日本の神々 : 神社と聖地 第4巻 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|矢田寺|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|航空神社|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|櫛玉命神社|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|磐船神社 (交野市)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|船山神社|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|石船神社 (村上市)|Q124496786}} === 外部リンク === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage ロコどこ 観光ナビ 矢田坐久志玉比古神社] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

street address (P6375) == * {'text': '日本 奈良県大和郡山市矢田町965', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市矢田町字東良', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} [[JA:矢田坐久志玉比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}{{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}})
{{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (large), former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}}, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{jalink|Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the '{{jalink|Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}'s consort {{jalink|Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{jalink|Kinai|Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{jalink|1879|Q7783}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{jalink|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}{{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}})
{{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (large), former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}}, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{jalink|Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the '{{jalink|Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}'s consort {{jalink|Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{jalink|Kinai|Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{jalink|1879|Q7783}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{jalink|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (large), former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}}, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{jalink|Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the '{{jalink|Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}'s consort {{jalink|Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{jalink|Kinai|Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{jalink|1879|Q7783}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{jalink|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}{{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}})
{{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (large), former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}}, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{jalink|Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the '{{jalink|Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}'s consort {{jalink|Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{jalink|Kinai|Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{jalink|1879|Q7783}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{jalink|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

{{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}{{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}})
{{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (large), former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|Yata-chō}}, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{jalink|Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{jalink|Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{jalink|Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|Daidō}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}) in the '{{jalink|Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495|Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}}'s consort {{jalink|Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{jalink|Kinai|Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} {{jalink|Soejimo District|Q11560415}} listed in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' and is an {{jalink|Shikinaisha|Engishiki-listed large shrine}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{jalink|1879|Q7783}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{jalink|Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}. It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It appears that the {{jalink|Meiji government}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{jalink|1938|Q18645}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{jalink|kensha}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{jalink|propeller|Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{jalink|fighter aircraft|Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{jalink|Yamatai|Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. One-bay {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{jalink|Homudawake-no-Mikoto}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} - Deities: {{jalink|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{jalink|Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{jalink|Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{jalink|otabisho|Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}}: Deity: {{jalink|Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|Kashihara Line}} {{jalink|Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Yatadera|Q1781626}} * {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine|Q30319396}} * {{jalink|Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926}} * {{jalink|Funayama Shrine|Q119929665}} * {{jalink|Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786}} === External links === * [http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' and is an {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. It appears that the {{ill||lt=Meiji government|UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources ===
