It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市矢田町字東良', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} [[JA:矢田坐久志玉比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} * {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|lt=Aviation Shrine|Q30319396|WD=Q30319396|1=Aviation Shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271|WD=Q54153271|1=Kushitamanomikoto Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926|WD=Q11587926|1=Iwafune Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Funayama Shrine|Q119929665|WD=Q119929665|1=Funayama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786|WD=Q124496786|1=Iwafune Shrine}} === External links === * |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市矢田町字東良', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} [[JA:矢田坐久志玉比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271|WD=Q54153271|1=Kushitamanomikoto Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926|WD=Q11587926|1=Iwafune Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Funayama Shrine|Q119929665|WD=Q119929665|1=Funayama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786|WD=Q124496786|1=Iwafune Shrine}} === External links === * 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市矢田町字東良', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} [[JA:矢田坐久志玉比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584205}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine |画像 = [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社-神門.jpg|270px|Gate tower]]
Gate tower |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}{{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}} 965 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}})
{{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (large), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = Yaochi Shrine, Yaochi Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = October 9, 10 |神事 = September 20: Aviation Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yata ni Imasu Kushi-
tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = tamahiko Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:矢田坐久志玉比古神社ー神門２.jpg|thumb|right|Propeller dedicated in the gate tower]] '''Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine''' (やたにいますくしたまひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yata Village (Nara Prefecture)|Q17173675|WD=Q17173675|lt=Yata-chō|1=Yata}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed as a large shrine, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = }} === See also === {{Commonscat|Yatanimasukushitamahiko-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Kushitama-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q54153271|WD=Q54153271|1=Kushitamanomikoto Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Katano)|Q11587926|WD=Q11587926|1=Iwafune Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Funayama Shrine|Q119929665|WD=Q119929665|1=Funayama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786|WD=Q124496786|1=Iwafune Shrine}} === External links === * It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. It appears that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji government|1=UNKNOWN}} applied pressure to have Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto enshrined for approximately 40 years. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * It is also known as Yaochi Shrine or Yaochi Myōjin[http://yamatokoriyama.locodoco.net/sightseeing/sightseeing-cate03/402.html#firstPage Loco-Doko Tourism Navi: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities - Kushitamahiko-no-Kami ({{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}), {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}} === History === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},' the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} flew through the sky riding the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwafune|Q126914107|WD=Q126914107|1=Ama-no-iwafune}} (Heavenly Rock Ship). It is said that the second arrow that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Kami shot from heaven landed at the spot where this shrine was built. Furthermore, the area came to be called "Yata" (arrow-field) because of this event. In the entry for divine estate grants of various shrines dated {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Shinchō Kakushikifushō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}},' it states "Yata Shrine, two households, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}," confirming that the shrine is an ancient institution predating that recordNara Prefecture History reference. Additionally, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s consort {{ill|lt=Yata no Himemiko|Q10892311|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}} is thought to have come from this areaFrom 'Shikinaisha no Kenkyū,' Volume 2. The shrine is regarded as the greatest ancient shrine of the region; from its founding through the first half of the 6th century, it is said to have prospered as the foremost celebrated shrine in the entire {{ill|lt=Kinai|Q906708|WD=Q906708|1=Kinai}} region. It is the head shrine among the ten shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} {{ill|lt=Soejimo District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In the Shrine Registry of {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), the shrine's deity was listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}. In the Meiji era, the shrine was ranked as a gōsha, but in October of {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Because of the tradition that Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto flew through the sky on the Ama-no-Iwafune, Kushitama-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is revered as the ancestral deity of aviation, and an Aviation Festival is held every year on September 20[https://www.yk-kankou.jp/spotDetail24.html Yamatokōriyama City Tourism Association: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. In the upper story of the gate tower, a {{ill|lt=propeller|Q922654|WD=Q922654|1=Propeller (aeronautics)}} said to have been used on a {{ill|lt=fighter aircraft|Q127771|WD=Q127771|1=Fighter aircraft}} has been dedicated and placed on display. Within the precinct there is a Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound). There is also a theory that this very area is the site of {{ill|lt=Yamatai|Q844295|WD=Q844295|1=Yamatai}}. === Precinct === * Honden ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in the early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. One-bay {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roofing[https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.lg.jp/soshiki/machidukuri_senryaku/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai101/j_bunkazai/kenzobutsu/1869.html Yamatokōriyama City Cultural Properties: Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine]. * Shinmon (sacred gate) * Haiden * Ni-no-ya-zuka (Second Arrow Mound) * Rōmon (gate tower) * Shamusho (shrine office) === Auxiliary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rebuilt in the mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} - Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796}} * Outlying shrines ** Nushito Shrine - Deities: {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, {{ill|lt=Mikashikiyahime|Q11341880|WD=Q11341880|lt=Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Mikashikiya-hime}}. Alternatively, one theory holds the deities are Mikashikiyahime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|WD=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (あめのひりとめのみこと), but it appears that Ame-no-Hiritome-no-Mikoto was only temporarily enshrined due to Meiji government pressure. Being both an outlying shrine and an {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} (sacred resting place for the portable shrine), considering its role as otabisho, the deities appear to be the two kami Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Mikashikiyahime-no-MikotoConfirmed with the chief priest. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}: Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo|1=UNKNOWN}}. Currently independent. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine, 2 structures{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.yamatokoriyama.nara.jp/ourcity/history/shiseki/000192.html |title=List of Important Cultural Properties |publisher=Yamatokōriyama City |accessdate=2019-08-24 }} ** Honden ** Massha Hachiman Shrine building === Festivals === * September 20 - Aviation Festival * October 9, 10 - Annual festival === Address === * Nara Prefecture, Yamatokōriyama City, Yata-chō 965 === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kashihara Line|1=Kashihara Line}} {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kōriyama Station|Q6414349|WD=Q6414349|1=Kintetsu-Kōriyama Station}}, take a bus bound for "Yamato-Koizumi Station East Exit" or "{{ill|lt=Yatadera|Q1781626|WD=Q1781626|1=Yata-dera Temple}} Mae," alight at "Yokoyama-guchi," then approximately 6 minutes on foot. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = Ikeda Suenori et al. |editor = Tanigawa Ken'ichi, ed. |title = Nihon no Kamigami: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 4 |year = 1985 |publisher = {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} |isbn = 4-560-02214-3 |page = Shrine|Q119929665|WD=Q119929665|1=Funayama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Iwafune Shrine (Murakami)|Q124496786|WD=Q124496786|1=Iwafune Shrine}} === External links === *
